dee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dewoin.
deed: noun. An action or act; something that is done.
deem: verb. To hold in belief or estimation; to adjudge as a conclusion; to regard as being; to evaluate according to one's beliefs; to account.
deemed: verb. simple past and past participle of deem
deme: noun. A township or other subdivision of ancient Attica.
den: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Slavey. (macrolanguage)
dene: noun. A valley, especially the deep valley of a stream or rivulet.
dent: noun. A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
dented: verb. simple past and past participle of dent
eme: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emerillon.
emend: verb. To correct and revise (text or a document).
emended: verb. simple past and past participle of emend
end: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ende.
ended: verb. simple past and past participle of end
med: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Melpa.
meed: noun. A payment or recompense made for services rendered or in recognition of some achievement; reward; award.
meet: verb. To make contact (with someone) while in proximity.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).
mend: verb. To physically repair (something that is broken, defaced, decayed, torn, or otherwise damaged).
mended: verb. simple past and past participle of mend
met: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mato.
mete: verb. To dispense, measure in order to dispense, allot (especially punishment, reward etc.).
meted: verb. simple past and past participle of mete
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
need: noun. A requirement for something; something needed.
neem: noun. Azadirachta indica, a large, mostly evergreen tree from India, whose seeds yield neem oil, the insecticide azadirachtin.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
ted: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tepo Krumen.
tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teem: verb. To be stocked to overflowing.
teemed: verb. simple past and past participle of teem
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tend: verb. To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
tended: verb. simple past and past participle of tend
ded: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dedua.
deen: noun. religion; religiosity; the way of life of a pious Muslim
dem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dem.
denet: verb. To reduce the price of (a book) below its agreed-upon net value with the publisher.
dmd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Madhi Madhi.
eden: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Eden (“a paradise”).
mee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mengen.
meeten: verb. To make or become suitable.
mene: noun. The high middle singing voice (especially as for a boy) or part in musical composition, corresponding roughly to the alto.
ment: verb. simple past and past participle of meng
mentee: noun. A person who is being mentored.
mtd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mualang.
ned: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nde-Gbite.
neet: noun. Alternative letter-case form of NEET.
nete: noun. In Ancient Greek musical theory, the highest-pitched fixed note in the farther tetrachord on a lyre, always pitched a perfect fourth

teened: verb. simple past and past participle of teen
tem: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Temne.
teme: noun. A meme which lives in a technological artifact rather than the human mind.
